AUDITOR’S DEPARTMENT.

Auditor.—Walter Carew Cocks, Esq.

Senior Inspector.—Edmund Baden, Esq.

Junior Inspector.—William Godsell, Esq.

Senior Examiners.—Frederick C. Holiday, Robert Shortrede, Alfred Benger, and William A. St. Quintin, Esqs.

Junior ditto.—Herbert W. Harding, Stephen H. Everett, Henry D. Poulton, and Frederick W. Crookshank, Esqs.

Second Class Clerks.—Charles Harman, Joseph Hodgson, William P. Horspool, Albert H. J. Neatham, and Francis S. Hutchings, Esqs.

STORE DEPARTMENT, BRANCH OFFICE,

AND DEPOT.

Belvedere Road, Lambeth, S.E.

Superintendent of General Supplies and Shipments.—J. Davies, Esq.

Assistant Superintendent of General Supplies and Shipments.—Capt. C. M. Watson, R.E.

Superintendent of Military Supplies.—Lieut., Col. C. Hunter, R.A.

Supervisors, First Grade.—Gerald H. Talbot, George Arnold, George McKeon, and James Graham, Esqs.

Supervisors, Second Grade.—W. Edgell, W. H. Hooker, D. E. Sinclair, and J. Byatt, Esqs.

Second Class Clerks.—H. C. Card, T. B. Couche, W. M. Biden, H. B. Walker, S. K. Letheren, H. G. Beard, A. G. Taunton, M. Kelly, and E. R. Ford, Esqs.

Examiners of Cloth.—Messrs. C. Beard, and R. H. Buck.

Examiners of Stores.—Messrs. J. Clayton, W. Chambers, G. Nott, J. W. Stone, G. Garratt, T. Weeks, J. O’Neill, W. McDonald, and W. Weeks.

Assistant Do.—Messrs. W. Tassell, H. G. Forbes, H. Diblin, E. J. Stone, R. May, R. W. Sadler, R. G. Barnes, G. May, G. S. Francis, R. Amsden, and O. Chapman.

Shipping Foreman and Custom House Agent.—Mr. T. Melvey.

Inspector of Scientific Instruments.—Thomas Cushing, Esq.

Examiner of Surgical Instruments.—G. D. Pollock, Esq.

Examiner of Veterinary Instruments.—Professor H. Simmonds.

Messengers.—G. Pile and T. Hodges.

STORE DEPARTMENT, STAMP BRANCH

Bunhill Row, E.C.

Superintendent.—W. H. Hosking, Esq.

Assistant Superintendents of Stamps.—Messrs. W. J. Raven and T. Gay.

Examiners of Stamps.—Messrs. G. Peters, C. Gay, G. Hobson, and W. J. Raven, jun.

HOME ESTABLISHMENT.INDIAN TROOP SERVICE vid EGYPT.—Temporary Arrangement.

The business of the Troop Service is under the superintendence of Admiral Sir W. R. Mends, K.C.B., Director of Transport Services at the Admiralty.

Consulting Officer for the Indian Troop Service.—Captain C. J. Cruttenden, late I.N.

Transport Officer, (Suez), and Agent to the Government of India in Egypt.—Commander A. W.

Chitty, late I.N.

Clerk at Suez.—C. Turner, Esq.

Resident Transport Officer (Bombay).—Lieut. G. O'B. Carew, late I.N.

Assistant do.—Lieut H. Morland, late I.N.

ROYAL INDIAN ENGINEERING COLLEGE, COOPER'S HILL.BOARD OF VISITORS.CHAIRMAN.

Sir Richard Temple, Bart., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., D.C.L.

MEMBERS.

W. H. Barlow, Esq., F.R.S., President, Institution of Civil Engineers

F. J. Bramwell, Esq., F.R.S.

Lieut.-Gen. C. H. Dickens, C.S.I., R.A.

Sir Barrow H. Ellis, K.C.S.I.

J. Fowler, Esq., Past President, Institution of Civil Engineers

STAFF OF THE COLLEGE.

President.—Gen. Sir A. Taylor, K.C.B., R.E.

Professor of Construction.—Callcott Reilly, Esq., M.I.C.E.

Professor of Hydraulic Engineering and Mechanism.—W. C. Unwin, Esq., Bac. Sc., M.I.C.E.

Assistant Professor of Engineering.—A. H. Heath, Esq., Assoc. Inst. C.E.

Professor of Mathematics.—Rev. J. Wolstenholme, M.A.

Professor of Applied Mathematics.—G. M. Minchin, Esq., M.A.

Professor of Surveying.—Lieut. Col. W. H. Edgecome, R.E.

Professor of Experimental Science.—Herbert McLeod, Esq.

Instructor in Surveying.—Maj. E. H. Courtney, R.E.

Instructor in Geometrical Drawing and Lecturer in Architecture.—T. H. Eagles, Esq., M.A.

Assistant Instructor in Geometrical Drawing.—

Instructor in Freehand Drawing.—S. T. G. Evans, Esq.

Lecturer in Hindustani and History of India.—Cotton Mather, Esq.

Lecturer in Geology and Mineralogy.—Professor P. M. Duncan, M.B., F.R.S.

Bursar.—J. P. Pasco, Esq., R.N., Retired.

Secretary.—J. G. Whiffin, Esq., R.N., Retired.

Chaplain.—Rev. C. Crosslé, B.D.

THE MOST EXALTED ORDER OF THE
STAR OF INDIA,†

INSTITUTED 1861. ENLARGED 1866, 1875, and 1876.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER.

The QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER OF THE ORDER.

His Excellency (THE MARQUIS OF RIPON, K.G., G.M.I.E.), the VICEROY and GOVERNOR
GENERAL of INDIA for the time being.

Knights Grand Commanders.

ORDER OF THE INDIAN EMPIRE.

INSTITUTED 1ST JANUARY, 1878.†

Consisting of Ex-Officio and Ordinary Companions.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER,

The QUEEN.

GRAND MASTER OF THE ORDER.

The VICEROY and GOVERNOR GENERAL of INDIA for the time being.

(His Excellency The MARQUESS OF RIPON, K.G., G.M.S.I.)

IMPERIAL ORDER OF THE CROWN OF INDIA.

INSTITUTED 1st JANUARY, 1878.

SOVEREIGN OF THE ORDER,—The QUEEN.

LADIES OF THE ORDER.—C.I.

(With Dates of Appointments).

Her Royal Highness the Princess of Wales, 1 Jan. 78 Her Highness Nawab Kudsia Begum, of Bhopal 1 Jan. 78

INDIA.

VICEROY AND GOVERNOR-GENERAL OF INDIA, The Most Hon. The MARQUIS OF RIPON, K.G., G.C.B., G.M.S.I.

Took his seat 8th June, 1880.

Military Secretary.—Lt. Col. G. S. White, C.B., V.C., 92nd Foot.

MEMO.—The Superintendents of the Central Jails (except Allahabad) are also Superintendents of the District Jails at the same stations. The remaining District Jails are usually in charge of the Civil Surgeons of the several Districts.

NOTE.—The names, in Italics, of Officers of the Staff Corps (who are borne for promotion on a separate List) are retained upon this list for the purpose of regulating the promotion of Officers of the Indian Army, —See Page 197.

Royal Cypher within the Garter, "Peninsula"—"Punniar"—"Sobraon"—"Punjab"—"Chillianwallah"—"Goojerat"—"Delhi"—"Lucknow"—"Charasia"—"Kabul, 1879"—"Kandahar, 1880"—"Afghanistan, 1878-80."

Blue. Facings Scarlet.—Pl. Black and White.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 10th February, 1875. Umballa.

Depôt at Canterbury.

BRITISH INFANTRY
2nd (Queen's Royal).

SECOND BATTALION.

The Paschal Lamb.

"Pristinæ virtutis memor"—"Vel exuvie triumphant."

The Royal Cypher within the Garter.

"Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—"Vimiera"—"Corunna"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Pyrenees"
"Nivelle"—"Toulouse"—"Peninsula"—"Affghanistan"—"Ghuzee"
"Khelat"—"Taku Forts"—"Pekin."

Landed at Bombay from Malta, 1st March, 1878.—Peshawar.

Brigade Depôt,—Guildford, Sub-District No. 48.

BRITISH INFANTRY.5th (Northumberland Fusiliers).SECOND BATTALION.

"Quo Fata Vocant"—St. George and the Dragon—The Rose and Crown—The King's Crest—"Wilhelmstah"
"Koleia"—"Vimiera"—"Corunna"—"Busaco"—"Ciudad Rodrigo"—"Badajoz"—"Salamanca"—
"Vitoria"—"Nivelle"—"Orthes"—"Toulouse"—"Peninsula"—"Lucknow"—"Afghanistan, 1878-80."

Scarlet—Furtings Bright Green.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 3rd February, 1880. Agra.

Brigade Depôt,—Berwick-on-Tweed. Sub-District No. 1.

Rank and Names. Regimental Rank. Army Rank. Local Rank. Remarks.
COLONEL.

BRITISH INFANTRY.23rd (Royal Welsh Fusiliers).1st BATTALION.

"Ich dien."

The Prince of Wales's plume.—The Rising Sun.—The Red Dragon.—The White Horse.—"Minden."
"Egypt" (with the Sphinx).—"Corunna."—"Martinique."—"Albuhera."—"Badajoz."—"Salamanca."
"Vittoria."—"Pyrenees."—"Nivelle."—"Orthes."—"Toulouse."—"Peninsula."—"Waterloo."—
"Alma."—"Inkerman."—"Sevastopol."—"Lucknow."—"Ashantee."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 12th September, 1880.—Chakrata.

Brigade Depôt.—Wrexham, Sub-District No. 23.

BRITISH INFANTRY.
25th (King's Own Borderers).

FIRST BATTALION.

The Castle of Edinburgh—"Nisi Dominus frustra." The King's Crest—"In Veritate Religionis Confido."
The White Horse—"Nec aspera terrent." "Minden" "Egmont-op-Zee" "Egypt"
(with the Sphinx)—"Martinique"—"Afghanistan, 1875-80."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived at Bombay from England, 14th November 1875. Peshawar.

Brigade Dépôt—York, Sub-District No. 6.

BRITISH INFANTRY.33rd (Duke of Wellington's Regiment).

The Crest and Motto of the late Duke of Wellington. "Seringapatam"—"Waterloo"—"Alma"—
"Inkerman"—"Sevastopol"—"Abyssinia."

Scarlet.—Facings, Scarlet.

Arrived in India on the 1st December, 1875. Lucknow.

Brigade Depôt—Halifax, Sub-District No. 9.

BRITISH INFANTRY.Rifle Brigade (The Prince Consort's Own).FOURTH BATTALION.

"Copenhagen"—"Monte Video"—"Roleia"—"Vimiera"—"Corunna"—"Busaco"—"Barossa"—
"Fuentes d'Onor"—"Ciudad Rodrigo"—"Badajoz"—"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Nivelle"—
"Nive"—"Orthes"—"Toulouse"—"Peninsula"—"Waterloo"—"Alma"—"Inkerman"—
"Sevastopol"—"Lucknow"—"Ashantee"—"Ali Musjid"—"Afghanistan, 1878-79."

Green—Facings Black.

Arrived at Bombay from Europe, 25th November, 1873—Rawal Pindi.

Brigade Depôt,—Winchester, Brigade Nos. 53 and 54.

Rawal Pindi. ]

4th Regiment.

Formerly 6th Irregular Cavalry.

Uniform, scarlet—Facings, blue—Lace, gold.

Honorary standard, bearing a lion passant reguardant, for services in Sind, 1814.

“Afghanistan, 1879–80.”

The Plume of the Prince of Wales, the Rising Sun and the Red Dragon. "Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—
"Peninsula"—"Waterloo"—"Sevastopol"—"Central India."

Blue—Facings and Plume Scarlet.

Arrived in India on the 31st January, 1877. Secunderabad.—Arrived on the 5th February, 1877,
from England.

The Royal Crest within the Garter. The Prussian Eagle. "Douro"—"Talavera"—"Fuentes d'Onor"—
"Salamanca"—"Vittoria"—"Orthes"—"Peninsula"—"Punjab"—"Chillianwallah"—"Goojerat"—
"Persia"—"Central India."

Blue—Busby-Bag, yellow; Plume, white.

Arrived in India on the 7th February, 1876. On Field Service, South Africa—Arrived from Bangalore Mar. 1881.

"Egypt" (with the Sphinx)—"Martinique"—"Ava"—"Afghanistan"—"Ghuznee"—"Jellalabad"
(with a Mural Crown)—"Cabool, 1842"—"Sevastopol."

Scarlet—Facings Blue.

Arrived in India on the 14th March, 1878.

21st (Royal Scots Fusiliers) Regiment of Foot.1ST BATTALION.

The Thistle within the Circle. St. Andrew. "Nemo me impune lacessit." Royal Cypher and Crown.
"Bladensburg"—"Alma"—"Inkerman"—"Sevastopol."

Red.—Facings, Blue.

Received on the Madras Establishment 21 January, 1870. Secunderabad.—Arrived on the 28th Dec.
1877, from Rangoon.

Arrived at Bombay 10th September, 1880, from England.

“Queen’s Own” Sappers and Miners.

“Seringapatam”—“Java”—“Egypt”—“Assaye”—“Mahidpore”—“Nagpore”—“Ava”—“Lucknow”—
“Central India”—“Afghanistan, 1878–80.”

A, C, and E Companies, “Pegu.”

A Company bears on its appointments “Taku Forts,” and “Pekin.”

A, B, and F Companies bear on their appointments, a Dragon, wearing an Imperial Crown, with the word
“China.”

B Company bears on its appointments “Pegu” and “Persia.”

C Company bears on its appointments “Meeanee” and “Hyderabad, 1843.”

G, H, and K Companies “Abyssinia.”

K Company “Taku Forts.”

D “ Bangalore I “ Bangalore
E “ Bangalore K “ Bangalore

BOMBAY.

GOVERNOR.

The Right Honourable Sir JAMES FERGUSON, Bart., K.C.M.G.

Took his seat, 28 Apr. 1880.

NOTE.—Native names will be found under the first portion of the name, omitting the following words when used as titles:—Babu, Bakshi, Haji, Jemadar, Kazi, Khan Bahadur, Khan Sahib, Khwajah, Maharajah, Iaulvi, Mir, Mirza, Nawab, Pundit, Rai, Rai Bahadur, Rajah, Rao, Rao Bahadur, Rao Saheb, Seth, Shahzadah, haikh, Sirdar, Subadar, Syud, and Wazirzadah.
